title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node II
hints: Process the tree level by level. How can you use the next pointers of the current level to link nodes in the next level?
For each node, connect its children to the next available sibling. You can find this sibling by traversing the current level's next chain.
Maintain a dummy head for the next level. As you traverse the current level, append children to this dummy list, then move to the new level's head.
